commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=67f9dff9475b1fb54d144ca61bae2666368301ae)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens two tooling scripts by rejecting unsafe decimal `content-length` values and adjusts the kitchen-sink RPC POSIX process sampler to include a matching gateway root process.

PR surface: Tests +61, Other +7. Total +68 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regression from source inspection: the branch selects `rootCommandMatches` before `descendants`, while the unchanged adjacent fixture expects the largest child for a launcher root with matching needles.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair can preserve the existing child fallback while adding the direct-root case; the protected maintainer label blocks cleanup close, not this repair recommendation.

target.title: fix(test): harden script probe bounds
target.body:
Summary:
- harden the kitchen-sink RPC POSIX process sampler so direct gateway roots are sampled without overriding the existing gateway-title child fallback
- reject unsafe decimal `content-length` values before reading kitchen-sink RPC probe bodies
- reject unsafe avatar probe `content-length` values before reading Clawtributors avatar bodies

Verification:
- `node --check scripts/e2e/kitchen-sink-rpc-walk.mjs`
- `node --check --experimental-strip-types scripts/update-clawtributors.ts`
- focused direct Node repros for the POSIX root sampler, gateway-title fallback, and unsafe kitchen-sink `content-length`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean)
- Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kvggv467b8jgnemzyd97b79y`: fetched `qa-sre-rpc-kitchen-sink-20260619` in the full checkout, then ran `pnpm check:changed` with `lanes=tooling` successfully

Known proof gap:
- native Crabbox macOS run is blocked by broker auth/config: `provider=aws requires a configured Crabbox broker for OpenClaw proof`.
target.author: vincentkoc
